Which value of b makes the inequality 0.25b + 6.5 < 8.0 true? Choose between 5, 6, or 7.
Which value of y makes the inequality 3.2y - 4.4 > 8.4 true? Choose between 3, 4, or 5.
Which value of k makes the inequality 0.8k + 1.1 ≤ 5.9 true? Choose between 6, 7, or 8.
Which value of s makes the inequality 1.25s + 2.5 ≥ 7.5 true? Choose between 2, 3, or 4.
Which value of z makes the inequality 4.5 - 0.5z > 1.5 true? Choose between 5, 6, or 7.
Which value of d makes the inequality 12.6 - 0.9d ≤ 7.2 true? Choose between 4, 5, or 6.
Which value of v makes the inequality v ÷ 4 + 2.2 < 4.7 true? Choose between 9, 10, or 11.
A recipe uses 8 cups of noodles and 10 cups of sauce. Write the ratio of cups of sauce to cups of noodles.
A recipe requires a ratio of 2 cups of sesame seeds to 5 cups of pretzels. Write the ratio of cups of pretzels to cups of sesame seeds.
A trail mix recipe uses 3 cups of almonds and 7 cups of raisins. Write the ratio of cups of almonds to total cups of mix.
A punch recipe uses 4 cups of orange juice and 9 cups of water. Write the ratio of cups of water to cups of orange juice.
A snack mix uses 5 cups of cereal and 8 cups of peanuts. Write the ratio of cups of peanuts to total cups of snack mix.
A smoothie recipe uses 6 cups of yogurt and 2 cups of berries. Write the ratio of cups of berries to cups of yogurt.
A soup recipe uses 3 cups of carrots and 5 cups of potatoes. Write the ratio of cups of carrots to total cups of vegetables.
A cookie recipe uses 7 cups of flour and 4 cups of sugar. Write the ratio of cups of sugar to cups of flour.
A salad recipe uses 6 cups of lettuce and 9 cups of tomatoes. Write the ratio of cups of tomatoes to total cups of salad.
A drink mix uses 2 cups of syrup and 11 cups of water. Write the ratio of cups of syrup to total cups of drink mix.
A granola recipe uses 8 cups of oats and 3 cups of honey. Write the ratio of cups of honey to cups of oats.

A recipe uses 10 cups of rice and 4 cups of beans. Write the ratio of cups of beans to cups of rice.
A lemonade recipe uses 3 cups of lemon juice and 12 cups of water. Write the ratio of cups of water to total cups of lemonade.
A salsa recipe uses 7 cups of tomatoes and 2 cups of onions. Write the ratio of cups of onions to total cups of salsa.
A cake recipe uses 9 cups of flour and 5 cups of milk. Write the ratio of cups of milk to cups of flour.
A cereal mix uses 4 cups of granola and 6 cups of dried fruit. Write the ratio of cups of granola to total cups of cereal mix.

A garden mix requires a ratio of 3 scoops of fertilizer to 8 gallons of water. How many scoops of fertilizer do we need for 24 gallons of water at the same ratio?
A map uses a ratio of 1 inch to 20 miles. How many inches on the map represent 100 miles at the same ratio?
A car travels 70 miles for every 2 gallons of gas. How many miles can the car travel with 8 gallons of gas at the same ratio?

Ben drives to two games. He drives for 6 minutes at an average speed of 70 miles per hour to reach the first game. He then drives for 24 minutes at an average speed of 55 miles per hour to reach the second game. During the 30 minutes of driving time, how many total miles does Ben drive?

Ryan delivers flowers to two customers. He drives for 12 minutes at an average speed of 40 miles per hour to reach his first customer. He then drives for 15 minutes at an average speed of 50 miles per hour to reach his second customer. During the 27 minutes of driving time, how many total miles does Ryan drive?

Mia drives to two stores. She drives for 10 minutes at an average speed of 30 miles per hour to reach the first store. She then drives for 20 minutes at an average speed of 45 miles per hour to reach the second store. During the 30 minutes of driving time, how many total miles does Mia drive?

Carlos delivers food to two houses. He drives for 18 minutes at an average speed of 50 miles per hour to reach the first house. He then drives for 12 minutes at an average speed of 40 miles per hour to reach the second house. During the 30 minutes of driving time, how many total miles does Carlos drive?

Emma visits two friends. She drives for 15 minutes at an average speed of 60 miles per hour to reach the first friend. She then drives for 10 minutes at an average speed of 48 miles per hour to reach the second friend. During the 25 minutes of driving time, how many total miles does Emma drive?

Noah delivers packages to two offices. He drives for 20 minutes at an average speed of 36 miles per hour to reach the first office. He then drives for 25 minutes at an average speed of 48 miles per hour to reach the second office. During the 45 minutes of driving time, how many total miles does Noah drive?

Lily drives to two parks. She drives for 9 minutes at an average speed of 40 miles per hour to reach the first park. She then drives for 18 minutes at an average speed of 30 miles per hour to reach the second park. During the 27 minutes of driving time, how many total miles does Lily drive?

Jacob makes two deliveries. He drives for 24 minutes at an average speed of 45 miles per hour to reach the first delivery. He then drives for 16 minutes at an average speed of 60 miles per hour to reach the second delivery. During the 40 minutes of driving time, how many total miles does Jacob drive?

Sophia drives to two appointments. She drives for 30 minutes at an average speed of 50 miles per hour to reach the first appointment. She then drives for 12 minutes at an average speed of 35 miles per hour to reach the second appointment. During the 42 minutes of driving time, how many total miles does Sophia drive?

Ethan drives to two schools. He drives for 14 minutes at an average speed of 60 miles per hour to reach the first school. He then drives for 21 minutes at an average speed of 40 miles per hour to reach the second school. During the 35 minutes of driving time, how many total miles does Ethan drive?

Ava delivers supplies to two classrooms. She drives for 25 minutes at an average speed of 24 miles per hour to reach the first classroom building. She then drives for 15 minutes at an average speed of 32 miles per hour to reach the second classroom building. During the 40 minutes of driving time, how many total miles does Ava drive?
There are red balloons and blue balloons at a party. The ratio of red balloons to blue balloons is 4 : 6. There are 14 more blue balloons than red balloons. How many red balloons are at the party?
There are red tiles and blue tiles in a box. The ratio of red tiles to blue tiles is 3 : 5. There are 12 more blue tiles than red tiles in the box. How many red tiles are in the box?
There are boys and girls in a club. The ratio of boys to girls is 4 : 7. There are 15 more girls than boys in the club. How many boys are in the club?
There are cats and dogs at an animal shelter. The ratio of cats to dogs is 2 : 9. There are 35 more dogs than cats. How many cats are at the shelter?
There are green marbles and yellow marbles in a bag. The ratio of green marbles to yellow marbles is 5 : 8. There are 21 more yellow marbles than green marbles. How many green marbles are in the bag?

There are small boxes and large boxes in a storage room. The ratio of small boxes to large boxes is 7 : 11. There are 24 more large boxes than small boxes. How many small boxes are in the storage room?
There are pencils and pens in a drawer. The ratio of pencils to pens is 6 : 10. There are 16 more pens than pencils. How many pencils are in the drawer?
There are roses and tulips in a garden. The ratio of roses to tulips is 3 : 4. There are 9 more tulips than roses. How many roses are in the garden?
There are apples and oranges in a basket. The ratio of apples to oranges is 5 : 9. There are 28 more oranges than apples. How many apples are in the basket?
There are trucks and cars in a parking lot. The ratio of trucks to cars is 2 : 5. There are 18 more cars than trucks. How many trucks are in the parking lot?
There are black beads and white beads in a box. The ratio of black beads to white beads is 8 : 13. There are 30 more white beads than black beads. How many black beads are in the box?

A painter painted 28 square feet of a wall. That area represents 40% of the total wall area. What is the total area of the wall?

A group of 10 Science Club students is on a field trip. That number of students represents 20% of the total number of students in the Science Club. What is the total number of students in the Science Club?
A bookstore sold 18 mystery books in one day. That number of books represents 30% of the total books sold that day. What is the total number of books sold?
A runner completed 24 miles this week. That number of miles represents 40% of the total miles in her training goal. What is the total number of miles in her goal?
A class collected 15 cans for a food drive. That number of cans represents 25% of the total cans collected by the grade. What is the total number of cans collected?
A farmer picked 36 apples in the morning. That number of apples represents 60% of the total apples picked that day. What is the total number of apples picked?
A student answered 8 questions correctly. That number of questions represents 16% of the total questions on the test. What is the total number of questions on the test?
A bakery sold 27 cupcakes before noon. That number of cupcakes represents 45% of the total cupcakes sold that day. What is the total number of cupcakes sold?
A water tank has 12 gallons of water in it. That amount represents 15% of the tank’s total capacity. What is the total capacity of the tank?
A soccer player made 35 passes during a game. That number of passes represents 70% of the team’s total passes. What is the total number of passes?
A library checked out 42 fiction books. That number of books represents 75% of all books checked out. What is the total number of books checked out?
A student spent 16 minutes reading. That amount of time represents 20% of the total time she studied. What is the total study time?
A store sold 54 shirts on Saturday. That number of shirts represents 90% of the shirts sold over the weekend. What is the total number of shirts sold over the weekend?
A recipe uses 14 ounces of flour. That amount represents 35% of the total weight of the ingredients. What is the total weight of the ingredients?
A theater sold 22 student tickets. That number of tickets represents 55% of all tickets sold. What is the total number of tickets sold?
A bike rider traveled 48 kilometers. That distance represents 80% of the full route. What is the total length of the route?
A jar contains 6 red marbles. That number of marbles represents 12% of all marbles in the jar. What is the total number of marbles in the jar?
A worker packed 30 boxes in the morning. That number of boxes represents 50% of the boxes packed that day. What is the total number of boxes packed?
A school printed 72 flyers. That number of flyers represents 60% of the total flyers needed. What is the total number of flyers needed?
A team scored 45 points in the first half. That number of points represents 75% of the total points scored in the game. What is the total number of points scored?
All the students at a park either played soccer or basketball. 75% of the students played soccer. 60 students played basketball. How many students were at the park?

All the students in the sixth grade either purchased their lunch or brought their lunch from home on Monday. 24% of the students purchased their lunch. 190 students brought their lunch from home. How many students are in the sixth grade?
All the students in a school either ride the bus or walk to school. 35% of the students ride the bus. 156 students walk to school. How many students are in the school?
All the animals at a shelter are either cats or dogs. 40% of the animals are cats. 90 animals are dogs. How many animals are at the shelter?
All the shirts in a store are either red or blue. 28% of the shirts are red. 144 shirts are blue. How many shirts are in the store?
All the students going on a field trip either paid online or paid in cash. 15% of the students paid online. 204 students paid in cash. How many students are going on the field trip?
All the books checked out from a library are either fiction or nonfiction. 62% of the books are fiction. 76 books are nonfiction. How many books were checked out?
All the plants in a garden are either tomato plants or pepper plants. 45% of the plants are tomato plants. 55 plants are pepper plants. How many plants are in the garden?
All the people at a concert are either adults or children. 30% of the people are adults. 210 people are children. How many people are at the concert?
All the students who answered a survey either chose yes or no. 68% of the students chose yes. 80 students chose no. How many students answered the survey?
All the students in a club are either girls or boys. 55% of the students are girls. 90 students are boys. How many students are in the club?
All the people at a gym either used treadmills or used exercise bikes. 12% of the people used treadmills. 176 people used exercise bikes. How many people were at the gym?
All the orders at a restaurant were either dine-in or takeout. 72% of the orders were dine-in. 84 orders were takeout. How many total orders were there?
All the players in a tournament were either on Team A or Team B. 20% of the players were on Team A. 128 players were on Team B. How many players were in the tournament?
All the donations for a fundraiser were either made online or in person. 36% of the donations were made online. 160 donations were made in person. How many total donations were made?
All the fruit in a basket is either apples or oranges. 25% of the fruit are apples. 225 pieces of fruit are oranges. How many pieces of fruit are in the basket?
All the students in a music program are either in band or choir. 48% of the students are in band. 130 students are in choir. How many students are in the music program?
At a school carnival, 5% of the prizes are large prizes, and 95% are small prizes. There are 300 prizes. How many prizes are large prizes?
In a school music concert, 20% of the audience are 7th graders, and 80% of the audience are 6th graders. There are 640 people in the audience. How many of them are 7th graders?
At a school play, 35% of the audience are parents, and 65% are students. There are 400 people in the audience. How many of them are parents?
In a basketball game, 25% of the fans are from School A, and 75% are from School B. There are 280 fans at the game. How many fans are from School A?
At a science fair, 40% of the visitors are teachers, and 60% are students. There are 350 visitors. How many visitors are teachers?
In a school assembly, 15% of the students are 8th graders, and 85% are 7th graders. There are 600 students in the assembly. How many students are 8th graders?
At a movie theater, 30% of the tickets sold are child tickets, and 70% are adult tickets. There are 250 tickets sold. How many child tickets were sold?
In a school club, 45% of the members are boys, and 55% are girls. There are 120 members in the club. How many members are boys?
At a food festival, 10% of the booths sell desserts, and 90% sell meals. There are 80 booths. How many booths sell desserts?
In a library, 60% of the books on a shelf are fiction, and 40% are nonfiction. There are 150 books on the shelf. How many books are fiction?
At a field trip, 75% of the students ride buses, and 25% ride vans. There are 160 students on the field trip. How many students ride buses?
In a soccer tournament, 50% of the players are in Group A, and 50% are in Group B. There are 96 players. How many players are in Group A?
At a charity event, 65% of the volunteers are adults, and 35% are teenagers. There are 200 volunteers. How many volunteers are adults?
In a school survey, 20% of students chose math, and 80% chose science. There are 450 students in the survey. How many students chose math?
At a pet shelter, 55% of the animals are dogs, and 45% are cats. There are 180 animals. How many animals are dogs?
In an art contest, 70% of the entries are drawings, and 30% are paintings. There are 110 entries. How many entries are drawings?
